TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_nipaluma-cisu,_ciso, title = nipaluma-cisu,_ciso, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/nipaluma-cisu,_ciso} }

S.FORM: cisu, ciso ś™Ąś®Ļ u siwkay nu nipaluma kagak kakuniza salaedanÁēĆ:ś§ćÁČ©ÁēĆ Plantae pananťĖÄ:ŤĘęŚ≠źś§ćÁČ©ťĖÄ Magnoliophyta upizÁ∂Ī:ÁúüťõôŚ≠źŤĎČś§ćÁČ© Eudicots mataÁõģ:ťĺćŤÜĹÁõģ Gentianales sapamatÁßĎ:ŤĆúŤćČÁßĎ Rubiaceae mikitinayŚĪ¨:ÁĺäŤßíŤó§ŚĪ¨ Morinda hicahicaayÁ®ģ:ś™Ąś®Ļ M. citrifolia u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika Morinda citrifolia is a tree in the coffee family, Rubiaceae. Its native range extends through Southeast Asia and Australasia, and the species is now cultivated throughout the tropics and widely naturalized. Among some 100 names for the fruit across different regions are the more common English names, great morinda, Indian mulberry, noni, beach mulberry, and cheese fruit.
S.PHON: ¶isu, ¶iso ** u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma kagak kakuniza sar~…ĺa…ôdan*:*** pr~…ĺanta…ô panan*:***** magnor~…ĺiopńßjta upiz*:****** …ôudi ¶ots mata*:*** g…ôntianar~…ĺ…ôs sapamat*:*** *ubia ¶…ôa…ô mikitinaj*:**** morinda ńßi ¶ańßi ¶aaj*:** m. ¶itrifor~…ĺia u san…ôk nu nipar~…ĺuma mar~…ĺańß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…ĺaŇč pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu-ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika morinda ¶itrifor~…ĺia is a tr…ô…ô in tńß…ô ¶off…ô…ô famir~…ĺj, *ubia ¶…ôa…ô. its nati*…ô raŇč…ô …ô*t…ônds tńßrougńß soutńß…ôast asia and austrar~…ĺasia, and tńß…ô sp…ô ¶i…ôs is now ¶ur~…ĺti*at…ôd tńßrougńßout tńß…ô tropi ¶s and wid…ôr~…ĺj naturar~…ĺiz…ôd. amoŇč som…ô *** nam…ôs for tńß…ô fruit a ¶ross diff…ôr…ônt r…ôgions ar…ô tńß…ô mor…ô ¶ommon …ôŇčr~…ĺisńß nam…ôs, gr…ôat morinda, indian mur~…ĺb…ôrrj, noni, b…ôa ¶ńß mur~…ĺb…ôrrj, and ¶ńß…ô…ôs…ô fruit.
S.FORM: cisu, ciso ś™Ąś®Ļ u siwkay nu nipaluma kagak kakuniza salaedanÁēĆ:ś§ćÁČ©ÁēĆ Plantae pananťĖÄ:ŤĘęŚ≠źś§ćÁČ©ťĖÄ Magnoliophyta upizÁ∂Ī:ÁúüťõôŚ≠źŤĎČś§ćÁČ© Eudicots mataÁõģ:ťĺćŤÜĹÁõģ Gentianales sapamatÁßĎ:ŤĆúŤćČÁßĎ Rubiaceae mikitinayŚĪ¨:ÁĺäŤßíŤó§ŚĪ¨ Morinda hicahicaayÁ®ģ:ś™Ąś®Ļ M. citrifolia u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika Morinda citrifolia is a tree in the coffee family, Rubiaceae. Its native range extends through Southeast Asia and Australasia, and the species is now cultivated throughout the tropics and widely naturalized. Among some 100 names for the fruit across different regions are the more common English names, great morinda, Indian mulberry, noni, beach mulberry, and cheese fruit.
